tags.name: `mc-input`
tags.description: Purpose: Component for user text input with various customization options. Category: Forms When to use: When you need to collect user input in a form. When not to use: When you need to display non-input content. Works with components: mc-accordion-item, mc-card, mc-dialog, mc-drawer, mc-modal, mc-popover, mc-step-indicator-item, mc-table, mc-tabs Alternative component names: Text Input, Form Input, Text Field, Input Box, Text Entry Events: * `input` {`InputEvent`} - Fired when the character is entered. * `focus` {`FocusEvent`} - Fired when mc-input is focused. * `blur` {`FocusEvent`} - Fired when mc-input is going out of focus. * `click` {`MouseEvent`} - Fired on mc-input click. * `keydown` {`KeyboardEvent`} - Fired when a key on keydown is pressed. * `clearbuttonclick` {`CustomEvent`} - Fires when the clear button is pressed. * `trailingiconclick` {`CustomEvent`} - Fires when the icon or trailing icon is clicked. Slots: * ``label`` {} - The label HTML to use for the mc-input. * ``hint`` {} - The hint HTML to use for the mc-input. * ``errormessage`` {} - The errormessage HTML to use for the mc-input. Attributes: * `valueAsNumber` {`number`} - Gets the value of the element interpreted as number, only if the input type is number. * `label` {`string`} - Label has to be passed as a property. It is required attribute as it will be used as aria-label. * `labelposition` {`LabelPosition`} - Position od the label and text alignment within label. * `placeholder` {`string`} - The `placeholder` attribute specifies a short hint that describes the expected value of a field (e.g. a sample value or a short description of the expected format). * `icon` {`string`} - Name of the icon. * `trailingicon` {`string`} - Name of the trailing icon. * `clickabletrailingicon` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, the trailingicon will be clickable. The iconclick event will be emitted on trailingicon click. * `trailingiconlabel` {`string | undefined`} - Set the title of the trailing icon to help users understand the action. * `minlength` {`number`} - Minimum number of characters required in the textarea. * `maxlength` {`number`} - Setting this property will activate a character counter and limit the number of characters that can be entered in the textarea. * `pattern` {`string`} - Specifies a regular expression the input value should match. * `min` {`Min`} - Minimum value of the input. * `max` {`Max`} - Maximum value of the input. * `step` {`Step`} - Step of the input. * `size` {`Size`} - Size of the input. * `prefix` {`Prefix`} - The prefix can have maximum 3 characters. You can either render prefix or an icon on the same side of the input. * `suffix` {`Suffix`} - The suffix can have maximum 3 characters. You can either render suffix or an icon on the same side of the input. * `inputmode` {`InputMode | undefined`} - `Inputmode` attribute of the input. This allows a browser to display an appropriate virtual keyboard. * `readonly` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `readonly` state. * `autocomplete` {`string`} - The HTML autocomplete attribute lets web developers specify what if any permission the user agent has to provide automated assistance in filling out form field values. * `autocapitalize` {`string`} - Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the user. * `autofocus` {`boolean`} - The autofocus global attribute is a Boolean attribute indicating that an element should be focused on page load, or when the that it is part of is displayed. * `hint` {`string`} - Hint can be passed as simple argument like: `hint="hint text"` or as a named slot: `hint text as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text. * `variant` {`Variant`} - Variant of the input * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the input. * `invalid` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the invalid state. * `id` {`string`} - Sets id for the input field. * `errormessage` {`string`} - Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like: `errormessage="error"` or as a named slot: `error message as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text. * `hiddenlabel` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only. * `width` {`string`} - The width of the inner input container in percentage. * `clearbutton` {`boolean`} - Whether to display a clear button. * `keepclearbuttonvisible` {`boolean`} - Keep clearbutton visible at all times (not only during input focus)', * `loading` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `loading` state. * `mask` {`string | Mask | undefined`} - The mask property must be a string or a RegExp. When using a string mask pattern, the following special characters can be used for defining a mask: 0 - any digit a - any letter * - any character [] - optional input {} - fixed part of a mask If a definition character should be treated as fixed it should be escaped by "\\" (E.g. \\0). You can also pass a mask as an object with a mask property. This does not have any benefit at the moment, but it will be used in the future to pass mask configuration. * `disabled` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `disabled` state. * `name` {`string`} - Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element. * `value` - The initial value of the input. * `checked` {`boolean | undefined`} - * `files` {`FileList | undefined`} - * `type` {`InputType`} - Type of the input. * `required` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `required` state. Properties: * `uniqueId` {`string`} - * `root` {`HTMLElement | null | undefined`} - * `_valueAsNumber` {`number`} - * `clearButtonEnabled` {`boolean`} - * `inputField` {`HTMLDivElement | null | undefined`} - * `handleInputClick` - * `inputLabel` {`string | Node`} - * `slottedInput` {`HTMLInputElement[] | null | undefined`} - * `defaultInputElement` {`HTMLInputElement | null | undefined`} - * `labelElements` {`Element[] | null | undefined`} - * `isInputFocused` {`boolean`} - * `isClearButtonFocused` {`boolean`} - * `isTrailingIconFocused` {`boolean`} - * `inputContainer` {`HTMLInputElement | null | undefined`} - * `inputId` {`string`} - * `maskController` - * `valueAsNumber` {`number`} - Gets the value of the element interpreted as number, only if the input type is number. * `label` {`string`} - Label has to be passed as a property. It is required attribute as it will be used as aria-label. * `labelposition` {`LabelPosition`} - Position od the label and text alignment within label. * `placeholder` {`string`} - The `placeholder` attribute specifies a short hint that describes the expected value of a field (e.g. a sample value or a short description of the expected format). * `icon` {`string`} - Name of the icon. * `trailingicon` {`string`} - Name of the trailing icon. * `clickabletrailingicon` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, the trailingicon will be clickable. The iconclick event will be emitted on trailingicon click. * `trailingiconlabel` {`string | undefined`} - Set the title of the trailing icon to help users understand the action. * `minlength` {`number`} - Minimum number of characters required in the textarea. * `maxlength` {`number`} - Setting this property will activate a character counter and limit the number of characters that can be entered in the textarea. * `pattern` {`string`} - Specifies a regular expression the input value should match. * `min` {`Min`} - Minimum value of the input. * `max` {`Max`} - Maximum value of the input. * `step` {`Step`} - Step of the input. * `size` {`Size`} - Size of the input. * `prefix` {`Prefix`} - The prefix can have maximum 3 characters. You can either render prefix or an icon on the same side of the input. * `suffix` {`Suffix`} - The suffix can have maximum 3 characters. You can either render suffix or an icon on the same side of the input. * `inputmode` {`InputMode | undefined`} - `Inputmode` attribute of the input. This allows a browser to display an appropriate virtual keyboard. * `readonly` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `readonly` state. * `autocomplete` {`string`} - The HTML autocomplete attribute lets web developers specify what if any permission the user agent has to provide automated assistance in filling out form field values. * `autocapitalize` {`string`} - Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the user. * `autofocus` {`boolean`} - The autofocus global attribute is a Boolean attribute indicating that an element should be focused on page load, or when the that it is part of is displayed. * `hint` {`string`} - Hint can be passed as simple argument like: `hint="hint text"` or as a named slot: `hint text as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text. * `variant` {`Variant`} - Variant of the input * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the input. * `invalid` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the invalid state. * `id` {`string`} - Sets id for the input field. * `errormessage` {`string`} - Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like: `errormessage="error"` or as a named slot: `error message as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text. * `hiddenlabel` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only. * `width` {`string`} - The width of the inner input container in percentage. * `clearbutton` {`boolean`} - Whether to display a clear button. * `keepclearbuttonvisible` {`boolean`} - Keep clearbutton visible at all times (not only during input focus)', * `loading` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `loading` state. * `setAriaLabel` - Sets aria label attribute of the mc-input. * `mask` {`string | Mask | undefined`} - The mask property must be a string or a RegExp. When using a string mask pattern, the following special characters can be used for defining a mask: 0 - any digit a - any letter * - any character [] - optional input {} - fixed part of a mask If a definition character should be treated as fixed it should be escaped by "\\" (E.g. \\0). You can also pass a mask as an object with a mask property. This does not have any benefit at the moment, but it will be used in the future to pass mask configuration. * `inputElement` {`HTMLInputElement | null | undefined`} - Internal protected input element in the mc-input. * `focus` - Sets focus to the mc-input. * `blur` - Sets focus out of the mc-input. * `click` - Clicks the mc-input. * `input` - Input in the mc-input. * `keydown` - Listens to the keydown event on the mc-input. This is useful for handling custom keyboard shortcuts or actions. * `clearbuttonclick` - Custom event emitted on clear button click (sends the whole option item as detail). * `focused` {`boolean`} - * `tabindex` {`number | undefined`} - * `disabled` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `disabled` state. * `_onMouseDown` - * `name` {`string`} - Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element. * `value` - The initial value of the input. * `checked` {`boolean | undefined`} - * `files` {`FileList | undefined`} - * `type` {`InputType`} - Type of the input. * `required` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `required` state. * `hiddenInput` {`HTMLInputElement | undefined`} - * `controlType` {`string`} - * `isCheckable` {`boolean`} - * `inputType` {`string`} -

Property: valueAsNumber

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
`label` {`string`} - Label has to be passed as a property.
It is required attribute as it will be used as aria-label.

Property: label

Default: Label

attributes.description:
`labelposition` {`LabelPosition`} - Position od the label and text alignment within label.

Property: labelposition

Default: top

Property: placeholder

Default:

attributes.description:
`inputmode` {`InputMode | undefined`} - `Inputmode` attribute of the input.
This allows a browser to display an appropriate virtual keyboard.

Property: inputmode

attributes.name: readonly
attributes.description:
`readonly` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `readonly` state.

Property: readonly

Default: false

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description:
`autocomplete` {`string`} - The HTML autocomplete attribute lets web developers specify
what if any permission the user agent has to provide automated assistance in filling out form field values.

Property: autocomplete

Default:

attributes.name: autocapitalize
attributes.description:
`autocapitalize` {`string`} - Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the user.

Property: autocapitalize

Default:

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description:
`autofocus` {`boolean`} - The autofocus global attribute is a Boolean attribute indicating that an element should be focused on page load, or when the <dialog> that it is part of is displayed.

Property: autofocus

Default: false

attributes.name: hint
attributes.description: `hint` {`string`} - Hint can be passed as simple argument like: `hint="hint text"` or as a named slot: `hint text as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text. Property: hint Default:

attributes.description:
`variant` {`Variant`} - Variant of the input

Property: variant

Default: default

attributes.name: fit

Property: fit

Property: invalid

Default: false

attributes.name: id
attributes.description:
`id` {`string`} - Sets id for the input field.

Property: id

Default:

attributes.name: errormessage
attributes.description: `errormessage` {`string`} - Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like: `errormessage="error"` or as a named slot: `error message as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text. Property: errormessage Default:

attributes.name: hiddenlabel
attributes.description:
`hiddenlabel` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only.

Property: hiddenlabel

Default: false

attributes.name: width
attributes.description:
`width` {`string`} - The width of the inner input container in percentage.

Property: width

Default: auto

attributes.name: clearbutton
attributes.description:
`clearbutton` {`boolean`} - Whether to display a clear button.

Property: clearbutton

Default: false

attributes.name: keepclearbuttonvisible
attributes.description:
`keepclearbuttonvisible` {`boolean`} - Keep clearbutton visible at all times (not only during input focus)',

Property: keepclearbuttonvisible

Default: false

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
`loading` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `loading` state.

Property: loading

Default: false

attributes.name: mask
attributes.description:
`mask` {`string | Mask | undefined`} - The mask property must be a string or a RegExp.
When using a string mask pattern, the following special characters can be used for defining a mask:
0 - any digit
a - any letter
* - any character
[] - optional input
{} - fixed part of a mask

If a definition character should be treated as fixed it should be escaped by "\\" (E.g. \\0).

You can also pass a mask as an object with a mask property.
This does not have any benefit at the moment, but it will be used in the future to pass mask configuration.

Property: mask

Default: undefined

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
`disabled` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `disabled` state.

Property: disabled

Default: false

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
`name` {`string`} - Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element.

Property: name

Default:

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
`value` - The initial value of the input.

Property: value

Default:

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description:
`checked` {`boolean | undefined`} - 

Property: checked

attributes.name: files
attributes.description:
`files` {`FileList | undefined`} - 

Property: files

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
`type` {`InputType`} - Type of the input.

Property: type

Default: text

attributes.name: required
attributes.description:
`required` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `required` state.

Property: required

attributes.name: oninput
attributes.description: `input` {`InputEvent`} - Fired when the character is entered.

attributes.name: onfocus
attributes.description: `focus` {`FocusEvent`} - Fired when mc-input is focused.

attributes.name: onblur
attributes.description: `blur` {`FocusEvent`} - Fired when mc-input is going out of focus.

attributes.name: onclick
attributes.description: `click` {`MouseEvent`} - Fired on mc-input click.

attributes.name: onkeydown
attributes.description: `keydown` {`KeyboardEvent`} - Fired when a key on keydown is pressed.

attributes.name: onclearbuttonclick
attributes.description: `clearbuttonclick` {`CustomEvent`} - Fires when the clear button is pressed.

attributes.name: ontrailingiconclick
attributes.description: `trailingiconclick` {`CustomEvent`} - Fires when the icon or trailing icon is clicked.

Use argument style for passing short hints, use named slot when you want to pass hint with HTML text. * `variant` {`Variant`} - Variant of the input * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the input. * `invalid` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the invalid state. * `id` {`string`} - Sets id for the input field. * `errormessage` {`string`} - Error message will be shown only if `invalid` attribute is set to `true`. Error message can be passed as simple argument like: `errormessage="error"` or as a named slot: `error message as HTML`. Use argument style for passing short messages, use named slot when you want to pass error message with HTML text. * `hiddenlabel` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only. * `width` {`string`} - The width of the inner input container in percentage. * `clearbutton` {`boolean`} - Whether to display a clear button. * `keepclearbuttonvisible` {`boolean`} - Keep clearbutton visible at all times (not only during input focus)', * `loading` {`boolean`} - Sets input in the `loading` state. * `setAriaLabel` - Sets aria label attribute of the mc-input. * `mask` {`string | Mask | undefined`} - The mask property must be a string or a RegExp. When using a string mask pattern, the following special characters can be used for defining a mask: 0 - any digit a - any letter * - any character [] - optional input {} - fixed part of a mask If a definition character should be treated as fixed it should be escaped by "\\" (E.g. \\0). You can also pass a mask as an object with a mask property. This does not have any benefit at the moment, but it will be used in the future to pass mask configuration. * `inputElement` {`HTMLInputElement | null | undefined`} - Internal protected input element in the mc-input. * `focus` - Sets focus to the mc-input. * `blur` - Sets focus out of the mc-input. * `click` - Clicks the mc-input. * `input` - Input in the mc-input. * `keydown` - Listens to the keydown event on the mc-input. This is useful for handling custom keyboard shortcuts or actions. * `clearbuttonclick` - Custom event emitted on clear button click (sends the whole option item as detail). * `focused` {`boolean`} - * `tabindex` {`number | undefined`} - * `disabled` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `disabled` state. * `_onMouseDown` - * `name` {`string`} - Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element. * `value` - The initial value of the input. * `checked` {`boolean | undefined`} - * `files` {`FileList | undefined`} - * `type` {`InputType`} - Type of the input. * `required` {`boolean | undefined`} - Sets the input in the `required` state. * `hiddenInput` {`HTMLInputElement | undefined`} - * `controlType` {`string`} - * `isCheckable` {`boolean`} - * `inputType` {`string`} -

attributes.name: valueAsNumber

It is required attribute as it will be used as aria-label.

attributes.name: mask
attributes.description:
`mask` {`string | Mask | undefined`} - The mask property must be a string or a RegExp.
When using a string mask pattern, the following special characters can be used for defining a mask:
0 - any digit
a - any letter
* - any character
[] - optional input
{} - fixed part of a mask

If a definition character should be treated as fixed it should be escaped by "\\" (E.g. \\0).

You can also pass a mask as an object with a mask property.
This does not have any benefit at the moment, but it will be used in the future to pass mask configuration.

Property: mask

Default: undefined

Default: false
